title: Digital Clock & Countdown timer - Final Touch

content.problemStatement:
Perfect - we have completed our Project!

Here are some additional tasks that you can try out.


Can you create a display box around the `digital_clock`. We have created one such version - check out the code in the IDE
Currently - you cannot exit the  timer or the clock - it keeps going on for every. 

Can you update the code to accept a user input to exit the `digital_clock` project?
constraints: Commands consist of printable ASCII characters
The clock starts at 00:00:00 and wraps after 23:59:59
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of commands. Each of the next T lines contains a command string without leading or trailing spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each command that is not exactly 'exit', output three lines forming a box: +-----------------------+, | hh:mm:ss |, +-----------------------+, where hh, mm, ss are two-digit hour, minute and second values of the current clock time. After printing, the clock advances by one second. If a command is 'exit', output a single line containing the word 'exit' and terminate processing further commands.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The only command is 'exit', so the program prints 'exit' and stops.
The command is not 'exit', therefore the current time (00:00:00) is displayed inside a box. After printing, the clock advances to 00:00:01 for any subsequent command.
First command shows 00:00:00, clock moves to 00:00:01. Second command shows 00:00:01, clock moves to 00:00:02. Third command is 'exit', so the program prints 'exit' and terminates.

hidden.label: stress/general

10
tick

tick

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by maintaining a digital clock that starts at 00:00:00 and increments by one second after each command. The clock wraps around after 23:59:59. For each command, check if it is 'exit'. If not, print the current time in the specified format and increment the clock by one second.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) per test case, O(T) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input, only a constant amount of space is used to store the clock time and the command.
